---
title: "Projects"
toc: false
---

<!--
This page is for short, evidence-backed project case studies.
Think “what I worked on and what came out of it”, not full lab notebooks 🙂
-->

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card}

## Mapping tau propagation across neural circuits

**Problem:**  
Alzheimer's pathology spreads across the brain in remarkably
predictable patterns, yet the cellular mechanisms guiding
this propagation remain poorly understood.

**My role:**  
Led the computational analysis of spatial transcriptomics
data from post-mortem brain samples.

**Approach:**  
Integrated spatial gene expression data with single-cell
RNA sequencing reference datasets to identify neuronal
populations most susceptible to early tau aggregation.

**Evidence:**

- Repo: https://github.com/dr-v-frankenstein/tau-spatial-analysis  
- Preprint: https://biorxiv.org  
- Dataset: https://zenodo.org  

:::
:::


::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card}

## Quantifying pathological protein aggregation

**Problem:**  
Different laboratories measure tau aggregation in brain tissue
using inconsistent methods, making studies difficult to compare.

**My role:**  
Developed a reproducible analysis pipeline for measuring
tau pathology from histology images.

**Approach:**  
Machine-learning based image segmentation combined with
high-resolution histology imaging and automated quantification.

**Evidence:**

- Repo: https://github.com/dr-v-frankenstein/tau-scoring-pipeline  
- Validation dataset: https://zenodo.org  
- Poster: UK DRI Annual Meeting  

:::
:::


::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card}

## Reconstructing cellular failure pathways in neurodegeneration

**Problem:**  
Why do some neurons succumb early in neurodegenerative disease
while neighbouring cells survive much longer?

**My role:**  
Designed the computational framework used to analyse
single-cell RNA sequencing datasets across disease stages.

**Approach:**  
Network modelling and gene-regulatory inference to reconstruct
molecular cascades leading to neuronal dysfunction.

**Evidence:**

- Repo: https://github.com/dr-v-frankenstein/neurodegeneration-networks  
- Preprint: https://biorxiv.org  

:::
:::


::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.card}

## Standardising measurements of neurodegenerative pathology

**Problem:**  
Measurements of protein aggregation and neuronal loss vary
widely between laboratories, making cross-study comparisons difficult.

**My role:**  
Coordinated a collaboration between several research groups
to benchmark different analysis approaches.

**Approach:**  
Developed shared analysis pipelines and standard datasets
to enable reproducible comparisons across labs.

**Evidence:**

- Repo: https://github.com/dr-v-frankenstein/neuro-benchmark  
- Workshop: UK DRI Computational Neuroscience meeting  

:::
:::

:::
